description_long: This is a cylindrical concrete bollard with a weathered, mottled gray surface, showing signs of age and exposure to the elements. The bollard features a thick, round cap and base, both slightly wider than the main shaft, giving it a sturdy and robust appearance. One side of the shaft has a large, irregularly shaped break or gouge, exposing a rough inner surface and some rust-colored streaks, possibly from rebar or internal metal reinforcement. The rest of the surface is pitted and stained, with small chips and cracks visible throughout, indicating long-term outdoor use. The overall form is utilitarian, designed to prevent vehicle access or demarcate pedestrian zones in urban environments. There are no visible reflective markings, chains, or connectors, and the cap is flat with a slight bevel at the edge.
description: A weathered, cylindrical concrete bollard with a flat, beveled cap and a large break exposing rusted interior. The surface is mottled gray, pitted, and stained, showing signs of age and outdoor use.
description_view_0: The bollard is seen with its large break and exposed interior partially visible on the right side.
description_view_1: This view highlights the main break in the shaft, showing rust-colored streaks and the rough inner surface.
description_view_2: The break is visible but less pronounced, with the cylindrical form and weathered texture dominating the view.
description_view_3: The unbroken side of the bollard is shown, displaying a continuous, mottled gray surface with no major damage.
